new_value: Completed: Added 36 boundary attack tests (NaN, Infinity, MAX, MIN, subnormal) to interaction_combinatorial_tests.rs. Fixed DSL panic bug. All tests pass. Note: Changes committed in default workspace due to workspace isolation workflow issue.
PR created: https://github.com/lprior-repo/Seshat/pull/4 - 36 boundary tests added, DSL panic fixed
PR created: https://github.com/lprior-repo/Seshat/pull/5

False positive: The 'mock' functions create real domain types (Node, CanvasState), not mock objects. This is standard test fixture pattern. Tests use actual domain types, not mocking frameworks. All 53 subgraph tests pass. No changes needed.

Not actionable: Test files are protected per TEST_PROTECTION.md (geometry tests are seshat-pnn contract). Naming convention is acceptable - doc comments provide BDD context (e.g., /// GEO-020-T010). Function names are descriptive shorthand.
Fixed unwrap in orthogonal_route (explicit match), fixed StoreBridge PoolNotInitialized to return proper errors, updated docs/00_CODEBASE_MAP.md with workspace structure, fixed AGENTS.md stale paths

Known limitation: orthogonal_route wrapper exists for backward compatibility. Error handling is visible in code (explicit match), not silent. Doc comments acknowledge this and provide migration path: 'For new code, prefer compute_orthogonal_route directly'. The TODO at line 187 documents the proper fix. This is not an active bug but a technical debt item with documented mitigation.
PR created: https://github.com/lprior-repo/Seshat/pull/6 - Enabled 4 runtime tests for StoreBridge
Fixed: Updated doc comment to honestly state error info is discarded. Commit 3be7756d6 on main.
Fixed: Added validation for obstacle coordinates. Commit 67f224d52 on main. Returns InvalidEndpoint if obstacle has NaN/Infinity.
{"assignee":"Lewis Prior","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"seshat-cdhy","title":"compute_orthogonal_route_avoiding: Missing validation for NaN obstacle coordinates","description":"The calc_detour_y and calc_detour_x_bounds functions assume obstacle coordinates are finite. If obstacle has NaN coordinates: 1) is_inside returns false (NaN comparisons are always false), 2) build_detour_route produces NaN points, 3) route_intersects returns false (NaN comparisons), 4) final route has NaN points. This is a latent bug in obstacle validation - not directly related to the orthogonal_route wrapper issue but found during adversarial testing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"priorlewis43@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-19T03:36:58Z","created_by":"Lewis.Prior","updated_at":"2026-03-19T11:43:50Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T11:43:50Z","close_reason":"Fixed: Added validation for obstacle coordinates. Commit 67f224d52 on main. Returns InvalidEndpoint if obstacle has NaN/Infinity."}
